page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the n1 family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, which corresponds to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding 0.9 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the lower performance tiers. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1086.39, categorized as weak, while the multi-core CPU performance drops to 1408.88, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only large block speeds at 13.22 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.3 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations, which reaches 1.35 million operations per second, similarly rank in the bottom 10%, reflecting the constrained memory and compute resources of this entry-level profile.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highcpu-2 offers a low-cost entry point for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation but minimal memory overhead. Due to its low resource density and low benchmark results across computational, memory, and database tests, it is not suitable for resource-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads are limited to lightweight background tasks, basic web serving, simple scripting, or small-scale development and testing environments.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-2 is an entry-level, compute-focused virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon x86_64 platform. Running at 2.3 GHz, the single physical core utilizes hyper-threading but lacks bundled local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmark results place this instance in the lower percentiles, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1086.39 and multi-core score of 1408.88. Memory bandwidth is constrained, reaching 13.22 GB/sec for large block reads. Given these hardware limitations, the server is best suited for low-resource, budget-conscious workloads. It is appropriate for basic web serving, lightweight background processes, and simple development tasks that do not require significant memory or high-performance multi-core processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-2 is an entry-level x86_64 server with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for lightweight, budget-conscious compute workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-2 is an entry-level x86_64 server with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM, suited for lightweight tasks.
tagline: An entry-level, budget-friendly compute instance with dedicated vCPUs and minimal memory for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 2.3 GHz processor
2.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
Low-tier benchmark performance across CPU and memory tests
Cost-effective option for dedicated CPU allocation with low RAM requirements
Best suited for lightweight background tasks, basic web serving, and testing
